TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: uzai a qam katua gang ka sitsuayan.
S.PHON: uzai a qam katua gaŋ ka siʦuajan.
S.FORM: uzai a qam katua gang ka sicuayan.
S.PHON: uzai a qam katua gaŋ ka siʦuajan.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time there were an ant-eater and a crab.

S.FORM: a gang katsuin na qam a kikaludjeɫ.
S.PHON: a gaŋ kaʦuin na qam a kikaɭuɉəʎ.
S.FORM: a gang kacuin na qam a kikaludjelj.
S.PHON: a gaŋ kaʦuin na qam a kikaɣuɉəʎ.
S.TRANSL: The ant-eater took the crab to get loquats.

S.FORM: qau kiɫavaran a gang: "imaza-u i teku.
S.PHON: qau kiʎavaran a gaŋ: "imaza-u i təku.
S.FORM: qau kiljavaran a gang: "imaza-u i teku.
S.PHON: qau kiʎavaRan a gaŋ: "imaza-u i təku.
S.TRANSL: The crab said: “Stay here below.

S.FORM: vaik aken a ɫevavaw, a yaya tua kaludjeɫ a sema vavaw," ayain a qam.
S.PHON: vaik akən a ʎəvavaw, a jaja tua kaɭuɉəʎ a səma vavaw," ajain a qam.
S.FORM: vaik aken a ljevavaw, a yaya tua kaludjelj a sema vavaw," ayain a qam.
S.PHON: vaik akən a ʎəvavaw, a jaja tua kaɣuɉəʎ a səma vavaw," ajain a qam.
S.TRANSL: I’ll go up and pick the fruit up at the top,” he said to the ant-eater.

S.FORM: sa vaik timadju a ɫevavaw.
S.PHON: sa vaik timaɉu a ʎəvavaw.
S.FORM: sa vaik timadju a ljevavaw.
S.PHON: sa vaik timaɉu a ʎəvavaw.
S.TRANSL: So he went up;

S.FORM: qau yayayain sa vuɫuvuɫuqan a paɫeteku.
S.PHON: qau jajajain sa vuʎuvuʎuqan a paʎətəku.
S.FORM: qau yayayain sa vuljuvuljuqan a paljeteku.
S.PHON: qau jajajain sa vuʎuvuʎuqan a paʎətəku.
S.TRANSL: and he picked the fruit and threw it down below.

S.FORM: qau a qam i teku; ɫuɫuɫuɫuin, sa pilipiliqi tua namalum.
S.PHON: qau a qam i təku; ʎuʎuʎuʎuin, sa piɭipiɭiqi tua namaɭum.
S.FORM: qau a qam i teku; ljuljuljuljuin, sa pilipiliqi tua namalum.
S.PHON: qau a qam i təku; ʎuʎuʎuʎuin, sa piɣipiɣiqi tua namaɣum.
S.TRANSL: The ant-eater was down below, picking them up and choosing out the ripe ones.

S.FORM: azua namalum patalataladjen tua umaq nimadju.
S.PHON: azua namaɭum pataɭataɭaɉən tua umaq nimaɉu.
S.FORM: azua namalum patalataladjen tua umaq nimadju.
S.PHON: azua namaɣum pataɣataɣaɉən tua umaq nimaɉu.
S.TRANSL: He put the ripe ones in his house;

S.FORM: qau avan anga matjaq sakamaya a qinpu nua qam.
S.PHON: qau avan aŋa macaq sakamaja a qinpu nua qam.
S.FORM: qau avan anga matjaq sakamaya a qinpu nua qam.
S.PHON: qau avan aŋa maʦaq sakamaja a qinpu nua qam.
S.TRANSL: and it was just the unripe ones that he collected together.

S.FORM: qau ɫeteku anga azua gang.
S.PHON: qau ʎətəku aŋa azua gaŋ.
S.FORM: qau ljeteku anga azua gang.
S.PHON: qau ʎətəku aŋa azua gaŋ.
S.TRANSL: The crab came down,

S.FORM: qau sa qenetji a qinpu nua qam.
S.PHON: qau sa qənəci a qinpu nua qam.
S.FORM: qau sa qenetji a qinpu nua qam.
S.PHON: qau sa qənəʦi a qinpu nua qam.
S.TRANSL: and had a look at what the ant-eater had collected.

S.FORM: mintu avan a matjaq sakamaya.
S.PHON: mintu avan a macaq sakamaja.
S.FORM: mintu avan a matjaq sakamaya.
S.PHON: mintu avan a maʦaq sakamaja.
S.TRANSL: It was only the unripe ones.

S.FORM: qau "sema inu anga a namalum?" aya azua gang.
S.PHON: qau "səma inu aŋa a namaɭumʔ" aja azua gaŋ.
S.FORM: qau "sema inu anga a namalum?" aya azua gang.
S.PHON: qau "səma inu aŋa a namaɣum?" aja azua gaŋ.
S.TRANSL: “Where have the ripe ones gone?” said the crab.

S.FORM: qau vaik azua gang.
S.PHON: qau vaik azua gaŋ.
S.FORM: qau vaik azua gang.
S.PHON: qau vaik azua gaŋ.
S.TRANSL: The crab went off.

S.FORM: qau a qam izuanan itjua qapulu nua kaludjeɫ.
S.PHON: qau a qam izuanan icua qapuɭu nua kaɭuɉəʎ.
S.FORM: qau a qam izuanan itjua qapulu nua kaludjelj.
S.PHON: qau a qam izuanan iʦua qapuɣu nua kaɣuɉəʎ.
S.TRANSL: The ant-eater was still at the foot of the tree.

S.FORM: qau lalapen nua gang.
S.PHON: qau ɭaɭapən nua gaŋ.
S.FORM: qau lalapen nua gang.
S.PHON: qau ɣaɣapən nua gaŋ.
S.TRANSL: The crab took (what was there).

S.FORM: mintu pinaɫetaladj anga tua umaq nua qam a namalum.
S.PHON: mintu pinaʎətaɭaɉ aŋa tua umaq nua qam a namaɭum.
S.FORM: mintu pinaljetaladj anga tua umaq nua qam a namalum.
S.PHON: mintu pinaʎətaɣaɉ aŋa tua umaq nua qam a namaɣum.
S.TRANSL: The ripe ones had all been put in the ant-eater’s house.

S.FORM: qau vaiken a sema zua nua gang.
S.PHON: qau vaikən a səma zua nua gaŋ.
S.FORM: qau vaiken a sema zua nua gang.
S.PHON: qau vaikən a səma zua nua gaŋ.
S.TRANSL: The crab went there.

S.FORM: mintu lialiaw a tsinpisan i taladj tua qinaman.
S.PHON: mintu ɭiaɭiaw a ʦinpisan i taɭaɉ tua qinaman.
S.FORM: mintu lialiaw a cinpisan i taladj tua qinaman.
S.PHON: mintu ɣiaɣiaw a ʦinpisan i taɣaɉ tua qinaman.
S.TRANSL: And there were lots of fruit he had picked lying in the ant-eater’s den.

S.FORM: qau "aku maitazua ti tja qali?" aya timadju.
S.PHON: qau "aku maitazua ti ca qaɭiʔ" aja timaɉu.
S.FORM: qau "aku maitazua ti tja qali?" aya timadju.
S.PHON: qau "aku maitazua ti ʦa qaɣi?" aja timaɉu.
S.TRANSL: “Why is our friend like that?” he said.

S.FORM: qau kisan kuya timadju, sa vaik a tjumaq.
S.PHON: qau kisan kuja timaɉu, sa vaik a cumaq.
S.FORM: qau kisan kuya timadju, sa vaik a tjumaq.
S.PHON: qau kisan kuja timaɉu, sa vaik a ʦumaq.
S.TRANSL: He was annoyed, and went off home.

S.FORM: qau tsuatsuay anga metsevung tiamadju a maḍusa.
S.PHON: qau ʦuaʦuaj aŋa məʦəvuŋ tiamaɉu a maɖusa.
S.FORM: qau cuacuay anga mecevung tiamadju a madrusa.
S.PHON: qau ʦuaʦuaj aŋa məʦəvuŋ tiamaɉu a maɖusa.
S.TRANSL: After a long time the two of them met again.

S.FORM: qau maɫavar tiamadju: "nangaq a qemaɫup itjen," aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau maʎavar tiamaɉu: "naŋaq a qəmaʎup icən," aja tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: qau maljavar tiamadju: "nangaq a qemaljup itjen," aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: qau maʎavaR tiamaɉu: "naŋaq a qəmaʎup iʦən," aja tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: They said to one another: “Let’s go hunting.”

S.FORM: "tiakenay anan a temalaw.
S.PHON: "tiakənaj anan a təmaɭaw.
S.FORM: "tiakenay anan a temalaw.
S.PHON: "tiakənaj anan a təmaɣaw.
S.TRANSL: “I’ll first lie in wait,

S.FORM: tisunay anan a ɫemama tua ɫaviavia," aya a qam.
S.PHON: tisunaj anan a ʎəmama tua ʎaviavia," aja a qam.
S.FORM: tisunay anan a ljemama tua ljaviavia," aya a qam.
S.PHON: tisunaj anan a ʎəmama tua ʎaviavia," aja a qam.
S.TRANSL: while you set fire to the grass,” said the ant-eater.

S.FORM: qau vaik a qam a sema taladj tua ɫaviavia.
S.PHON: qau vaik a qam a səma taɭaɉ tua ʎaviavia.
S.FORM: qau vaik a qam a sema taladj tua ljaviavia.
S.PHON: qau vaik a qam a səma taɣaɉ tua ʎaviavia.
S.TRANSL: The ant-eater went into the grass.

S.FORM: qau qemawqaw a gang: "inu anga sun?" ayain nua gang a qam.
S.PHON: qau qəmawqaw a gaŋ: "inu aŋa sunʔ" ajain nua gaŋ a qam.
S.FORM: qau qemawqaw a gang: "inu anga sun?" ayain nua gang a qam.
S.PHON: qau qəmawqaw a gaŋ: "inu aŋa sun?" ajain nua gaŋ a qam.
S.TRANSL: The crab shouted: “Where are you?” he said to the ant-eater.

S.FORM: "imaza ken," aya a qam.
S.PHON: "imaza kən," aja a qam.
S.FORM: "imaza ken," aya a qam.
S.PHON: "imaza kən," aja a qam.
S.TRANSL: “Here I am,” said the ant-eater.

S.FORM: "pai anga," ayain anan nua gang a qemawqaw.
S.PHON: "pai aŋa," ajain anan nua gaŋ a qəmawqaw.
S.FORM: "pai anga," ayain anan nua gang a qemawqaw.
S.PHON: "pai aŋa," ajain anan nua gaŋ a qəmawqaw.
S.TRANSL: “All right then,” shouted the crab again.

S.FORM: "mayanan," ayain nua qam.
S.PHON: "majanan," ajain nua qam.
S.FORM: "mayanan," ayain nua qam.
S.PHON: "majanan," ajain nua qam.
S.TRANSL: “Wait a bit,” said the ant-eater.

S.FORM: manu semuasuad tua umaq azua qam.
S.PHON: manu səmuasuad tua umaq azua qam.
S.FORM: manu semuasuad tua umaq azua qam.
S.PHON: manu səmuasuad tua umaq azua qam.
S.TRANSL: He scooped out a house;

S.FORM: qau maka suad azua qam tua umaq; ɫetaladj timadju.
S.PHON: qau maka suad azua qam tua umaq; ʎətaɭaɉ timaɉu.
S.FORM: qau maka suad azua qam tua umaq; ljetaladj timadju.
S.PHON: qau maka suad azua qam tua umaq; ʎətaɣaɉ timaɉu.
S.TRANSL: and when he had finished scooping it out he got inside.

S.FORM: qau qemawqaw tua gang: "pai anga.
S.PHON: qau qəmawqaw tua gaŋ: "pai aŋa.
S.FORM: qau qemawqaw tua gang: "pai anga.
S.PHON: qau qəmawqaw tua gaŋ: "pai aŋa.
S.TRANSL: Then he shouted to the crab: “All right now.

S.FORM: maka talaw anga ken," aya azua qam.
S.PHON: maka taɭaw aŋa kən," aja azua qam.
S.FORM: maka talaw anga ken," aya azua qam.
S.PHON: maka taɣaw aŋa kən," aja azua qam.
S.TRANSL: I’m ready lying in wait,” said the ant-eater.

S.FORM: qau ɫamain anga azua ɫaviavia nua gang.
S.PHON: qau ʎamain aŋa azua ʎaviavia nua gaŋ.
S.FORM: qau ljamain anga azua ljaviavia nua gang.
S.PHON: qau ʎamain aŋa azua ʎaviavia nua gaŋ.
S.TRANSL: Then the crab set fire to the grass.

S.FORM: qau qawqawan nua gang: "inu anga sun?" ayain.
S.PHON: qau qawqawan nua gaŋ: "inu aŋa sunʔ" ajain.
S.FORM: qau qawqawan nua gang: "inu anga sun?" ayain.
S.PHON: qau qawqawan nua gaŋ: "inu aŋa sun?" ajain.
S.TRANSL: And the crab shouted: “Where are you?”

S.FORM: "imaza ken," aya qam.
S.PHON: "imaza kən," aja qam.
S.FORM: "imaza ken," aya qam.
S.PHON: "imaza kən," aja qam.
S.TRANSL: “Here I am,” said the ant-eater.

S.FORM: "inu anga sun?" ayain nua gang uta.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa sunʔ" ajain nua gaŋ uta.
S.FORM: "inu anga sun?" ayain nua gang uta.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa sun?" ajain nua gaŋ uta.
S.TRANSL: “Where are you?” said the crab again.

S.FORM: "imaza ken," ayain nua qam uta.
S.PHON: "imaza kən," ajain nua qam uta.
S.FORM: "imaza ken," ayain nua qam uta.
S.PHON: "imaza kən," ajain nua qam uta.
S.TRANSL: “Here I am,” said the ant-eater again.

S.FORM: qau tsemalivat a sapui.
S.PHON: qau ʦəmaɭivat a sapui.
S.FORM: qau cemalivat a sapui.
S.PHON: qau ʦəmaɣivat a sapui.
S.TRANSL: The fire passed.

S.FORM: "inu anga sun?" ayain uta nua gang.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa sunʔ" ajain uta nua gaŋ.
S.FORM: "inu anga sun?" ayain uta nua gang.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa sun?" ajain uta nua gaŋ.
S.TRANSL: “Where are you?” said the crab again.

S.FORM: "imaza ken," aya a qam; sa ɫekatsauan tua umaq nimadju.
S.PHON: "imaza kən," aja a qam; sa ʎəkaʦauan tua umaq nimaɉu.
S.FORM: "imaza ken," aya a qam; sa ljekacauan tua umaq nimadju.
S.PHON: "imaza kən," aja a qam; sa ʎəkaʦauan tua umaq nimaɉu.
S.TRANSL: “Here I am,” said the ant-eater, and emerged from his home.

S.FORM: qau sa "tisunay anga a temalaw," ayain a gang.
S.PHON: qau sa "tisunaj aŋa a təmaɭaw," ajain a gaŋ.
S.FORM: qau sa "tisunay anga a temalaw," ayain a gang.
S.PHON: qau sa "tisunaj aŋa a təmaɣaw," ajain a gaŋ.
S.TRANSL: “Now it’s your turn to lie in wait,” the crab was told.

S.FORM: qau "muri a sema inu aken?" aya zua gang.
S.PHON: qau "muri a səma inu akənʔ" aja zua gaŋ.
S.FORM: qau "muri a sema inu aken?" aya zua gang.
S.PHON: qau "muRi a səma inu akən?" aja zua gaŋ.
S.TRANSL: “Where am I to go to?” said the crab.

S.FORM: tjumaɫan nua qam: "sazua-u itjua namaqurepung a ɫavia," ayain.
S.PHON: cumaʎan nua qam: "sazua-u icua namaqurəpuŋ a ʎavia," ajain.
S.FORM: tjumaljan nua qam: "sazua-u itjua namaqurepung a ljavia," ayain.
S.PHON: ʦumaʎan nua qam: "sazua-u iʦua namaquRəpuŋ a ʎavia," ajain.
S.TRANSL: The ant-eater told him: “Go in there in the tangled grass,” he said.

S.FORM: qau azua gang vaik a kipataluvtuv tua namaqurepung a ɫaviaviain.
S.PHON: qau azua gaŋ vaik a kipataɭuvtuv tua namaqurəpuŋ a ʎaviaviain.
S.FORM: qau azua gang vaik a kipataluvtuv tua namaqurepung a ljaviaviain.
S.PHON: qau azua gaŋ vaik a kipataɣuvtuv tua namaquRəpuŋ a ʎaviaviain.
S.TRANSL: So the crab went and curled up small in the place with tangled grass.

S.FORM: qau "pai anga," ayain nua qam.
S.PHON: qau "pai aŋa," ajain nua qam.
S.FORM: qau "pai anga," ayain nua qam.
S.PHON: qau "pai aŋa," ajain nua qam.
S.TRANSL: “All right then,” said the ant-eater.

S.FORM: "pai anga," aya a gang.
S.PHON: "pai aŋa," aja a gaŋ.
S.FORM: "pai anga," aya a gang.
S.PHON: "pai aŋa," aja a gaŋ.
S.TRANSL: “All right,” said the crab.

S.FORM: qau ɫamain nua qam.
S.PHON: qau ʎamain nua qam.
S.FORM: qau ljamain nua qam.
S.PHON: qau ʎamain nua qam.
S.TRANSL: The ant-eater set fire (to the grass).

S.FORM: "inu anga sun?" ayain nua qam.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa sunʔ" ajain nua qam.
S.FORM: "inu anga sun?" ayain nua qam.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa sun?" ajain nua qam.
S.TRANSL: “Where are you?” said the ant-eater.

S.FORM: "imaza ken," aya a gang.
S.PHON: "imaza kən," aja a gaŋ.
S.FORM: "imaza ken," aya a gang.
S.PHON: "imaza kən," aja a gaŋ.
S.TRANSL: “Here I am,” said the crab.

S.FORM: qau tsemalivat a sapui.
S.PHON: qau ʦəmaɭivat a sapui.
S.FORM: qau cemalivat a sapui.
S.PHON: qau ʦəmaɣivat a sapui.
S.TRANSL: The fire passed.

S.FORM: "inu anga sun?" ayain nua qam.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa sunʔ" ajain nua qam.
S.FORM: "inu anga sun?" ayain nua qam.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa sun?" ajain nua qam.
S.TRANSL: “Where are you?” said the ant-eater.

S.FORM: mintu nekanga.
S.PHON: mintu nəkaŋa.
S.FORM: mintu nekanga.
S.PHON: mintu nəkaŋa.
S.TRANSL: No reply.

S.FORM: "inu anga sun?" ayain anan uta.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa sunʔ" ajain anan uta.
S.FORM: "inu anga sun?" ayain anan uta.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa sun?" ajain anan uta.
S.TRANSL: “Where are you?” he said once again.

S.FORM: mintu nekanga.
S.PHON: mintu nəkaŋa.
S.FORM: mintu nekanga.
S.PHON: mintu nəkaŋa.
S.TRANSL: No reply.

S.FORM: qau vaiken sa kiqenetji nua qam a sema zua.
S.PHON: qau vaikən sa kiqənəci nua qam a səma zua.
S.FORM: qau vaiken sa kiqenetji nua qam a sema zua.
S.PHON: qau vaikən sa kiqənəʦi nua qam a səma zua.
S.TRANSL: The ant-eater went there to have a look.

S.FORM: mintu nakuḍesul anga namatsay anga.
S.PHON: mintu nakuɖəsuɭ aŋa namaʦaj aŋa.
S.FORM: mintu nakudresul anga namacay anga.
S.PHON: mintu nakuɖəsuɣ aŋa namaʦaj aŋa.
S.TRANSL: And the crab was stiff and dead.

S.FORM: qau tseviqan a qalitsi.
S.PHON: qau ʦəviqan a qaɭiʦi.
S.FORM: qau ceviqan a qalici.
S.PHON: qau ʦəviqan a qaɣiʦi.
S.TRANSL: He sliced off some of the crab’s penis.

S.FORM: qau sa kisenay a papuvaɫut.
S.PHON: qau sa kisənaj a papuvaʎut.
S.FORM: qau sa kisenay a papuvaljut.
S.PHON: qau sa kisənaj a papuvaʎut.
S.TRANSL: Then he chanted a spell and brought him back to life.

S.FORM: qau katsuin a qalitsi a tjumaq.
S.PHON: qau kaʦuin a qaɭiʦi a cumaq.
S.FORM: qau kacuin a qalici a tjumaq.
S.PHON: qau kaʦuin a qaɣiʦi a ʦumaq.
S.TRANSL: And he took the crab’s penis home.

S.FORM: qau kadjamadjaman anga.
S.PHON: qau kaɉamaɉaman aŋa.
S.FORM: qau kadjamadjaman anga.
S.PHON: qau kaɉamaɉaman aŋa.
S.TRANSL: Next morning,

S.FORM: qau sipakan anga a qalitsi nimadju nua qam.
S.PHON: qau sipakan aŋa a qaɭiʦi nimaɉu nua qam.
S.FORM: qau sipakan anga a qalici nimadju nua qam.
S.PHON: qau sipakan aŋa a qaɣiʦi nimaɉu nua qam.
S.TRANSL: the crab was given some of his own penis to eat by the ant-eater.

S.FORM: qau "ha ha; kanen a qalitsi nimadju," aya a qam.
S.PHON: qau "ha ha; kanən a qaɭiʦi nimaɉu," aja a qam.
S.FORM: qau "ha ha; kanen a qalici nimadju," aya a qam.
S.PHON: qau "ha ha; kanən a qaɣiʦi nimaɉu," aja a qam.
S.TRANSL: “Ha ha, he’s eating his own penis,” said the ant-eater.

S.FORM: qau kemɫang azua gang tu qalitsi nimadju azua kinan.
S.PHON: qau kəmʎaŋ azua gaŋ tu qaɭiʦi nimaɉu azua kinan.
S.FORM: qau kemljang azua gang tu qalici nimadju azua kinan.
S.PHON: qau kəmʎaŋ azua gaŋ tu qaɣiʦi nimaɉu azua kinan.
S.TRANSL: Then the crab knew that it was his own penis that he had eaten.

S.FORM: "ii, pakanan aken tua niaken a qalitsi ni ku qali," aya, sa djiladjilay a gang.
S.PHON: "ii, pakanan akən tua niakən a qaɭiʦi ni ku qaɭi," aja, sa ɉiɭaɉiɭaj a gaŋ.
S.FORM: "ii, pakanan aken tua niaken a qalici ni ku qali," aya, sa djiladjilay a gang.
S.PHON: "ii, pakanan akən tua niakən a qaɣiʦi ni ku qaɣi," aja, sa ɉiɣaɉiɣaj a gaŋ.
S.TRANSL: “Ugh! My friend has given me my own penis to eat,” said the crab and spat.

S.FORM: ɫakua kudain, kinan anga nimadju a qalitsi.
S.PHON: ʎakua kudain, kinan aŋa nimaɉu a qaɭiʦi.
S.FORM: ljakua kudain, kinan anga nimadju a qalici.
S.PHON: ʎakua kudain, kinan aŋa nimaɉu a qaɣiʦi.
S.TRANSL: But there was nothing for it; he had eaten some of his own penis.

S.FORM: qau tsemikel anga a qalitsi nua gang.
S.PHON: qau ʦəmikəɭ aŋa a qaɭiʦi nua gaŋ.
S.FORM: qau cemikel anga a qalici nua gang.
S.PHON: qau ʦəmikəɣ aŋa a qaɣiʦi nua gaŋ.
S.TRANSL: (Later) the crab’s penis grew back again.
